data.title: Meta discussed buying Perplexity before Scale AI investment - Bloomberg
data.description:
Meta Platforms (NASDAQ:META) held discussions with artificial intelligence search startup Perplexity AI about a possible takeover before going forward with its multibillion-dollar investment in Scale AI, reported Bloomberg News on Friday.

The two companies couldn’t come to an agreement and decided not to pursue the deal, the report said, citing people familiar with the matter.

Meta and Perplexity did not immediately respond to a request for comment.

Earlier this month, Meta announced a $14.3 billion investment in Scale, acquiring a 49% stake and valuing the San Francisco-based company at over $29 billion. A key aspect of the deal included Scale CEO Alexandr Wang joining Meta to head its “superintelligence” team focused on artificial general intelligence.

Scale AI, which was founded in 2016, provides access to a network of human trainers with specialized knowledge to generative AI model makers.

Meta’s aggressive push into superintelligence research, or as many call it, artificial general intelligence, or AGI is part of Mark Zuckerberg’s broader strategy to revitalize the company’s AI ambitions.

data.title: Nasdaq, S&P, Dow move lower despite Fed official hinting at a potential rate cut in July
data.description:
Wall Street’s major market averages are lower on Friday even as Federal Reserve Governor Christopher Waller hinted at potential rate cuts in July.

The benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) finds itself down 0.3%, the blue-chip Dow (DJI) is near even, and the tech focused Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) has drifted lower by 0.7%.

Across the S&P 500, five of the 11 sectors are trading in the green, with Consumer Staples and Energy posting the strongest gains while Communication Services has been the weakest performer so far.

U.S. Treasury yields have edged lower, with the shorter-end U.S. 2 Year Treasury yield (US2Y) sliding 4 basis points to 3.89% while the longer-end U.S. 10 Year Treasury yield (US10Y) is down 2 basis points tot 4.36%.

Today also marks the second triple witching event of the year—a day on which $6.5T worth of contracts tied to stock options, stock index futures and stock index options will expire. Such days have typically seen volatility in equity markets.

Waller said Friday he sees little risk that tariffs will stoke inflation and signaled the central bank could start cutting interest rates as soon as July.

On the geopolitical front President Donald Trump announced that he will decide within the next two weeks if the U.S. should launch an attack on Iran, pressuring Tehran to the negotiating table for a nuclear deal.

"Markets are recovering a bit this morning after White House spokeswoman Leavitt last night said that Trump had dictated a message saying that 'based on the fact that there's a substantial chance of negotiations that may or may not take place with Iran in the near future, I will make my decision whether or not to go within the next two weeks,'" Deutsche Bank's Jim Reid said.

That means two weeks of uncertainty for financial markets, but investors are still inclined to see the Middle East conflict as a local, not a global, economic issue, UBS' Paul Donovan said.

From an economic vantage point, the Philly Fed Manufacturing gauge stalled at -4 in June, missing consensus. Furthermore, the U.S. Leading Indicator figures inched down 0.1% to 99.0 in May, matching the consensus estimates.

data.title: Market Voices: July Fed rate cut eyed, GLP-1s, Darden, Aflac, JPMorgan
data.description:
Seeking Alpha's daily roundup of remarks and statements that could impact markets, sectors or individual stocks. 

  • Federal Reserve Governor Christopher Waller said Friday that the Fed could implement an interest rate cut as early as July, as increased tariffs don't appear to be significantly boosting inflation.

“I think we’re in the position that we could do this as early as July,” Waller said during an interview with CNBC. “That would be my view, whether the committee would go along with it or not.”

  • The American College of Cardiology is recommending that more obese patients take weight-loss drugs, such as Eli Lilly's (NYSE:LLY) tirzepatide and Novo Nordisk's (NVO) semaglutide, to help prevent cardiovascular events.

"Beyond improvement of CVD [cardiovascular disease] risk factors, third-generation obesity medications (NuSH therapies) have been shown to reduce adverse cardiovascular events among those with obesity and established atherosclerotic CVD and/or HFpEF," the group said in a clinical guidance document on its journal website. "Obesity management by the cardiovascular community needs to be embraced, given both the prevalence of obesity and the impact it has on many forms of CVD."

Tirzepatide and semaglutide belong to a class of drugs called GLP-1s that are used for diabetes control and weight loss.

  • Alfac (NYSE:AFL) said suspicious activity identified on its U.S. network on June 12 may have compromised customer data.

"We continue to serve our customers as we respond to this incident and can underwrite policies, review claims, and otherwise service our customers as usual," Aflac said in a statement.

"This attack, like many insurance companies are currently experiencing, was caused by a sophisticated cybercrime group. This was part of a cybercrime campaign against the insurance industry," Aflac added.

  • Darden Restaurants (NYSE:DRI) CEO Rick Cardenas said the company is looking for strategic alternatives for its Bahama Breeze chain. 

“After careful review, we’ve determined [the chain] is no longer a strategic priority and believe Bahama Breeze has the potential to benefit from a new owner,” Cardenas said on the company’s earnings call with analysts.

The restaurant operator released a better-than-expected fiscal fourth quarter earnings report Friday morning. 

  • JPMorgan (NYSE:JPM) is rolling out roll new tools for investors to research and buy bonds and certain CDs through its mobile app. 

“Our goal was to create an experience that makes it extremely simple for clients that want to buy fixed income,” said Paul Vienick, head of online investing for the bank's wealth management division, according to CNBC. “We’ve taken that exact thought process for the simplicity of [buying] stocks and ETFs and moved that into the fixed-income space.”

data.title: First Quantum makes progress towards Panama mine restart - Bloomberg
data.description:
First Quantum Minerals (OTCPK:FQVLF) has begun preparations to ship out 120K metric tons of semi-processed copper stranded since its Cobre Panama mine was shut in late 2023, Bloomberg reported Friday.

Also, Franco-Nevada (NYSE:FNV), which has a supply agreement for metals from the mine, has agreed to halt its arbitration case against Panama, a step forward towards an eventual restart operations at the mine given that dropping the arbitration was a government prerequisite for negotiations.

Panama President Mulino said Thursday that the slate is now clean to start talks about a potential restart, Bloomberg reported.

Mine workers and providers, as well as members of other unions and some local communities, announced a Sunday march to push for the resumption of Cobre Panama that would give some counterweight to public protests against mining that first led to its closure.

It is still not clear whether Panama's government will be able to navigate lingering anti-mining sentiment in order to resume operations for the $10B mine that once accounted for 1% of all global mined copper.

data.title: DoorDash sets 5 1/2-yr high, on track for 6th consecutive weekly gain
data.description:
Shares of DoorDash (NASDAQ:DASH) are set to close in the green for a sixth consecutive week, with a new 5 1/2-year high of $223.81 per share reached during Friday’s trading, albeit in light trading volume.

Year-over-year, the stock price has appreciated by 102% versus a 9% gain for the S&P 500. Short interest float currently stands at a very modest 4.4%.

DoorDash (NASDAQ:DASH) last reported quarterly results on May 6, at which time the company showed an 18% increase in total orders and 20% increase in Marketplace gross order value to $23.1B, beating the $22.9B consensus estimate. The combination pushed total revenue up ~21%.

Since then, the company announced the acquisition of UK food delivery firm Deliveroo, NYC-based software company Seven Rooms, and ad platform Symbiosys.

Analysts are split, however, regarding DoorDash (NASDAQ:DASH): while Seeking Alpha contributors generally rate the stock as a Hold, Wall Street analysts see DoorDash (DASH) a Buy.

Seeking Alpha analyst PropNotes highlighted the company’s consistent revenue growth, improving margins, and rising profitability, noting: “As DASH continues expanding into global markets and leveraging its duopoly in delivery, we see considerable upside for shareholders.”

In contrast, APAC Investment News rates the stock a Sell, citing DoorDash’s “high fees and luxury positioning,” which could make it vulnerable as “financially stressed consumers cut discretionary spending and seek better value alternatives.”

Seeking Alpha’s Quant views DoorDash as a Hold, with a score of 3.26 out of 5.

data.title: Canadian Natural to sell control of gas plant to gain approval for Schlumberger deal
data.description:
Canadian Natural Resources (NYSE:CNQ) agreed to sell 75% of its interest in the Seiu Lake natural gas processing plant in Alberta to secure antitrust clearance for its proposed acquisition of Schlumberger's (SLB) stake in the Palliser Block joint venture that includes control of 15 processing plants in the province, Canada's Competition Bureau said Friday.

The Bureau found the proposed transaction likely would result in a significant increase in market concentration in an area surrounding three natural gas processing plants - Seiu Lake, Wintering Hills and Wayne Dalum - all located ~130 km east of Calgary, which could lead to higher prices and fewer options for natural gas producers in that region.

To resolve the Bureau's concerns, Canadian Natural (NYSE:CNQ) agreed to sell a 75% interest in the Seiu Lake plant to privately-held North 40 Resources, which will become operator of the plant, leaving Canadian Natural with a 25% stake.

SLB (SLB) said last October it had agreed to sell its interests in the Palliser Block, including oil and gas wells, surface facilities, a pipeline network and certain oil and gas development rights.

data.title: U.S. plans to revoke waivers for allies’ chip plants in China: report
data.description:
The U.S. is planning to revoke waivers used by global semiconductor makers to access American technology in China, as per a recent report by the Wall Street Journal.

The move is likely to affect companies like South Korea’s Samsung Electronics (OTCPK:SSNLF) and SK Hynix (OTCPK:HXSCF) as well as Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (NYSE:TSM), who benefit from blanket waivers that permit them to export U.S. chip-making equipment to their Chinese factories without needing to obtain individual licenses for each shipment.

Jeffrey Kessler, head of the unit in charge of export controls at the Commerce Department, reportedly told the three companies this week that he wanted to cancel those waivers, calling the plan a part of President Donald Trump’s crackdown on the transfer of sensitive U.S. technology to China.         

The move is not meant to be a new trade escalation but is aimed at aligning the licensing requirements for chip equipment with China's system for rare-earth materials, the report said citing White House officials.

According to industry officials quoted by WSJ, Washington’s curbs may not lead to immediate factory shutdowns but could gradually hinder their ability to function efficiently.

In May, the U.S. and China agreed to a significant reduction in tariffs on each other’s goods for 90 days, calming the global market’s volatile run. Last week, officials from both countries agreed in principle on a framework intended to revive their trade truce and lift China’s export restrictions on rare earth elements. However, Washington’s latest measure could jeopardize the progress made by the two countries.

data.title: Health insurers to announce changes for prior authorizations: WSJ
data.description:
Health insurers are set to unveil major changes to overhaul their prior authorization requests as early as next week in a bid to smoothen the process that can determine whether a patient is eligible to receive healthcare coverage, the Wall Street Journal reported Friday.

Leading health insurers will pledge to create a common standard by the start of 2027 for submitting electronic requests for prior authorizations, also known as preauthorizations, people familiar with the matter said.

The initiative to be announced early next week also includes plans to answer 80% of electronic requests in real-time by 2027 only if submissions are forwarded with all the relevant documentation.

It comes amid complaints from the industry about the bureaucratic burden of prior authorizations, which require hospitals and doctors to obtain coverage approvals from health plans before initiating certain non-emergency medical procedures in patients.

A spokesperson for industry lobby group America's Health Insurance Plans (AHIP) didn’t immediately respond to requests for comments.

Selected health insurers: UnitedHealth (NYSE:UNH), Humana (HUM), CVS Health (NYSE:CVS), Cigna (NYSE:CI), Clover Health (CLOV), Alignment Healthcare (ALHC), Centene (CNC), Molina Healthcare (MOH), and Elevance Health (NYSE:ELV).

data.title: European markets rise; UK market falls on disappointing retail sales data
data.description:
Update: This article reflects European markets closing indices.

London (UKX) -0.20% at 8,775.

UK retail sales fall sharply in May.  

Germany (DAX:IND) +1.27% at 23,351.

France (CAC:IND)  +0.48% at 7,590.

The pan-European Stoxx 600 (STOXX) rose +0.13% at 563.5 on Friday. Investors kept a close eye on the conflict in the Middle East between Iran and Israel, and potential direct U.S. involvement.

Eutelsat (OTCPK:EUTLF) shares rose after the European satellite operator announced a 1.35 billion euro ($1.55 billion) capital raise backed by the French state and other shareholders.

In the bond market, the U.S. 10-year Treasury yield was down 2 basis points at 4.37%.

Germany's 10-year yield was unchanged at 2.51%.

The UK's 10-year yield was down 2 basis points at 4.53%.

Currencies: (EUR:USD) (GBP:USD) (CHF:USD)

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:EWG), (NYSE:GF), (NYSEARCA:EWI), (NYSEARCA:EWQ), (NASDAQ:FGM), (NASDAQ:DAX), (NYSEARCA:FLGR), (NYSEARCA:FXB), (NYSEARCA:EWU), (NASDAQ:FKU), (BATS:EWUS), (NYSEARCA:FLGB), (NYSEARCA:GREK)

Seeking Alpha Editor Khushi Singh contributed to this article.

data.title: Five Point to acquire controlling interest in business, operations of Hearthstone
data.description:
Five Point Holdings (NYSE:FPH) announced on Friday the acquisition of a controlling interest in the business and operations of the capital solutions provider to homebuilders, Hearthstone.

The California-based mixed-use planned communities developer has entered into a definitive agreement for the acquisition of 75% of a newly formed entity, Hearthstone Residential Holdings, that will include substantially all of the business and operations of Hearthstone.

The remaining 25% is set to be retained by entities affiliated with Hearthstone CEO Mark Porath. Hearthstone will continue to be led by its existing management team.

The terms of the acquisition, expected to close by the end of the third quarter, were not disclosed.
